title: Add media-kind and prompt-contains filters to artifacts page
description: Add two server-side filters to /artifacts: (1) media kind = video/image/other derived from primary artifact, (2) prompt-contains substring search over metadata.prompt only. URL-state per project convention. Python-side filter on parsed block_results JSON.
design:
Decisions:
- Media kind source: primary artifact (matches card preview).
- Media kind buckets: video / image / other.
- Prompt search fields: metadata.prompt only (not negative_prompt or other text).
- Backend impl: Python-side filter on parsed block_results JSON; no schema migration. Fine for personal-tool scale.
- URL state via search params (project convention from CLAUDE.md).

title: Multi-video generation + stitcher block
description:
Enable generating multiple videos sequentially in a single block run (Wan 2.2 already supports this via newline-separated prompts) and stitch the ordered outputs into one combined video via a new downstream Video Stitcher block.

ASK
- 'Director Mode' on Wan blocks: >1 prompts, submitted in parallel, ordered outputs preserved.
- New downstream 'Video Stitcher' block: uses ffmpeg to combine an ordered list of videos into one. Supports crossfade effects. Re-render on settings change.
- User also raised an alternative: a kitchen-sink 'Video Director' block (stitch + speed + slow + LUT).

KEY DISCOVERIES
- wan_22_image_to_video already splits prompt on newlines, submits in parallel via Promise.allSettled, polls via startNewPendingPoll which preserves submission order (serverless-poller.ts:180 — submitted.map(entry => jobMap.get(entry.idx))). Output is setOutput('video', artifacts) — an ORDERED array.
- So Director Mode is largely a UX/labeling change, not a backend change. The real new work is the stitcher block.
- PORT_VIDEO is already an array-capable port used by image_viewer/video_viewer style consumers.

DECISION TREE (in progress — to be filled during grilling)
design:
DECISIONS (from grilling 2026-05-15)

Q1 Block shape → Build BOTH a dedicated video_stitcher AND a separate video_fx block.
  Why: composable, single-purpose, matches existing convention.

Q2 ffmpeg runtime → Local FastAPI subprocess.
  Why: Wan poller already saves files locally (local_video_url). Cheap CPU work.
  Path: /api/blocks/video_stitcher/run, /api/blocks/video_fx/run.

Q3 Director Mode UX on Wan blocks → Toggle + per-prompt editor (add/remove/reorder rows).
  When OFF: existing textarea. When ON: row list, internally → string[].
  When prompt input is wired upstream: force OFF.
  Backend: no change — poller already preserves submission order (serverless-poller.ts:180).

Q4 Director Mode scope → Wan 2.2 i2v + t2v ONLY (not comfy_gen — its automation axes are a different concept).

Q5 Crossfade UI → Curated transition dropdown (None/Fade/Slide/Wipe/Circle/Pixelize) + duration slider 0.1–2.0s (default 0.5). Single transition between all pairs.

Q6 Stitch trigger / audio → Auto-stitch on first pipeline run. Manual "Re-stitch" button afterwards. Drop audio (Wan outputs are silent).

Q7 FX ops → Speed, LUT (.cube), Loop/boomerang. (No standalone Reverse; boomerang covers it.)

Q8 FX I/O → video[] → video[], order preserved, applied per-clip.

ASSUMPTIONS (no explicit answer; flagged in chat)
- LUT input: free-form path text field for v1 (no folder scanning).
- Stitched output dir: output/stitched/<timestamp>_<hash>.mp4

OUT OF SCOPE
- Per-pair transitions, audio, reverse-as-standalone, comfy_gen director mode, remote ffmpeg.

INVARIANT
- Order preservation must hold across Wan→fx→stitcher chain.
  Wan: submitted.map(e => jobMap.get(e.idx)) (serverless-poller.ts:180)
  fx: Promise.all over input array, index-assembled
  stitcher: ffmpeg filter graph references inputs positionally [0:v][1:v]…
notes:
Implemented on worktree feat+director-mode-and-video-blocks:
- custom_blocks/video_stitcher/{backend,frontend}.block.{py,tsx}
- custom_blocks/video_fx/{backend,frontend}.block.{py,tsx}
- Director Mode toggle + per-prompt editor in custom_blocks/wan_22_image_to_video/frontend.block.tsx
- _register.ts regenerated (auto via predev)
- TS build passes (npm run build).
- ffmpeg 8.0.1 available locally.
- Not yet smoke-tested end-to-end (needs a Wan run with multiple prompts + downstream stitcher); awaiting user manual test.
